source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/Clinical Image Report/009_Clinical Image Report - A1717MCW.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for A1717MCW against comparative radiographic equipment using DICOM 3.0 image acquisition.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by comparing radiographic image quality, clinical visibility, and expert conclusions for A1717MCW.
keyPoints: The objective device is A1717MCW by H&abyz and the comparative equipment is HAD1717MCW made by H&Abyz.
Image acquisition is stated as DICOM 3.0 across the reviewed clinical image views.
The report compares 30 plain radiographic image views and lists total average scores of 3.68 for HAD1717MCW and 3.71 for A1717MCW.
The conclusion states that images obtained with A1717MCW are comparable or superior to the same view obtained from a similar patient with HAD1717MCW.
The selected clinical evaluation expert is Professor Sung Hyun Kim, M.D., Ph.D., described as a board-certified radiologist in Korea.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: FDA is referenced as a requirement in the text, but the document appears to be an internal manufacturer clinical image report rather than an FDA-issued document.
Conflicting model evidence appears in the Knee AP section, which lists HAD1417MCW and F1417MCW while the document header and most sections identify A1717MCW and HAD1717MCW.
OCR quality issues are present, including inconsistent company spelling, typographical errors such as DCIOM, and malformed wording.
